old_claim.note: Bicycle provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Bicycle-derived lightweight vehicle practice, especially wire-spoked wheels and chain/belt drive layouts, helped enable Benz-style motor-vehicle prototypes without making bicycles the sole path.

source_shape.source_locator: The Benz and Cie. / Patent-Motorwagen section says Benz used similar technology from his bicycle experience, including wire wheels and chain/drive arrangements.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source links Benz's automobile design to bicycle experience and identifies wire wheels plus chain/drive features.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports an enabling edge only for lightweight wheel and drive practice, not for the full bicycle as a required precursor to every automobile path.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: automobile_prototype keeps bicycle as an enabling edge with a narrowed component rationale.
would_reject_if: The automobile_prototype node were narrowed away from Benz-style lightweight three-wheeled prototypes.
A stronger source showed Benz's wheel and drive choices had no bicycle-derived design relationship.
A future graph split added a more precise wire-spoked-wheel or lightweight-chain-drive node.
short_reason: Bicycle-derived wheel and drive practice supports the prototype edge.
